rationale: The citing paper discusses Chandrashekaran et al. (2007) in detail in the introduction, summarizing its mechanism and findings to motivate the focal problem of customer vulnerability despite high satisfaction. In the theory development, it uses that paper as part of the mean-variance foundation and to justify predictions about satisfaction heterogeneity. In the discussion, it explicitly compares its own results to the 2007 study and frames its contribution as moving from individual-level loyalty effects to firm-level shareholder-value implications. This is clearly a meaningful functional citation, not a passing background mention.
relation_description: The citing study builds directly on Chandrashekaran et al. (2007)'s satisfaction-strength logic and extends it to a firm-level model linking satisfaction level and heterogeneity to shareholder value and volatility. It preserves the core idea that variance around satisfaction matters, while adding a new outcome domain and longitudinal industry setting.
